rootCauseHypothesis:
The test is finding the "4" text, but the click is being blocked by a sibling/parent div with classes like "gradient-border hover-lift fade-in step-glow" that intercepts pointer events. This suggests a front-end layering/z-index/pointer-events issue or an animation overlay that covers the target during or after render. On the hosting side, the deployed build likely has a CSS/DOM overlap bug where a decorative or loading overlay remains above the intended clickable element, causing Playwright to time out because the click cannot reach the text element. Underlying error: TimeoutError: Locator.click: Timeout 10000ms exceeded. Call log: - waiting for get_by_text("4", exact=True) - locator resolved to 4 - attempting click action - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting 20ms 2 × waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is not stable - retrying click action - waiting 100ms - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is not stable 16 × retrying click action - waiting 500ms - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting 500ms - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable

recommendedFixTarget.rationale: Inspect the step/card UI around the element containing text "4" and ensure no overlapping layer or animated wrapper is sitting on top of the clickable target. The likely fix is to make the number/card itself the click target with a proper button role, or set the decorative overlay to pointer-events: none so it cannot intercept clicks. Also verify that any fade/step-glow animation completes before interaction and that the element does not remain in an unstable/transient state. If the clickable region is intentionally inside a parent card, attach the click handler to the parent and avoid nested absolutely positioned overlays blocking pointer events.
